questions.q: Which fundamental Kubernetes networking requirement is TRUE about pod-to-pod communication?
options: Pods on different nodes must use NAT to communicate with each other.
Every pod gets a unique IP and can communicate with any other pod directly without NAT.
Pods can only communicate with pods on the same node without NAT.
Pod-to-pod communication requires a Service resource to route traffic.
questions.explanation: Kubernetes mandates that every pod has a unique cluster-wide IP and that all pods can reach all other pods directly without NAT — regardless of which node they are on. This is the fundamental CNI contract.

questions.q: When a new pod is created, which component is responsible for calling the CNI plugin to set up pod networking?
questions.explanation: kubelet is the node agent that manages the pod lifecycle. After the pod is scheduled and created on a node, kubelet calls the configured CNI plugin to set up the pod's network namespace, veth pair, IP allocation, and routing.

questions.q: Flannel provides richer NetworkPolicy enforcement capabilities compared to Cilium.
questions.explanation: False. Flannel is a simple overlay network (VXLAN-based) with limited NetworkPolicy support. Cilium uses eBPF technology and provides advanced L3/L4/L7-aware network policies, observability, and higher performance.
